# chronosphere_slo (Resource)

A service-level objective that tracks the ratio of good to total events produced by an SLI over a configured time window, with optional multi-window burn-rate alerting.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_team" "payments" {
  name = "Payments"
}

resource "chronosphere_blackhole_alert_notifier" "blackhole" {
  name = "Blackhole"
}

resource "chronosphere_notification_policy" "np" {
  name    = "SLO policy"
  team_id = chronosphere_team.payments.id

  route {
    severity  = "warn"
    notifiers = [chronosphere_blackhole_alert_notifier.blackhole.id]
  }
}

resource "chronosphere_collection" "c" {
  name    = "Payments"
  team_id = chronosphere_team.payments.id
}

resource "chronosphere_slo" "request_success" {
  name                   = "payments request success"
  collection_id          = chronosphere_collection.c.id
  notification_policy_id = chronosphere_notification_policy.np.id

  definition {
    objective = 99.95
    time_window {
      duration = "28d"
    }
    enable_burn_rate_alerting = true
  }

  sli {
    custom_indicator {
      bad_query_template   = "sum(rate(http_request_duration_seconds_count{error=\"true\"}[{{ .Window }}]))"
      total_query_template = "sum(rate(http_request_duration_seconds_count[{{ .Window }}]))"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `collection_id` (String) ID of the collection that owns this SLO.
- `definition` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) SLO definition specifying the objective, time window, and burn-rate alerting configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--definition))
- `name` (String) Display name of the SLO. Can be changed after creation.
- `sli` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Service-level indicator (SLI) definition that produces the good/bad event ratio measured by this SLO. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sli))

### Optional

- `annotations` (Map of String) Free-form key/value pairs included in notifications generated by this SLO. Templated against signal labels.
- `description` (String) Free-form description of the SLO.
- `labels` (Map of String) Key/value labels attached to the SLO. Visible in notifications and usable for routing overrides in notification policies.
- `notification_policy_id` (String) ID of the notification policy used to route burn-rate alerts from this SLO. If omitted, the policy of the parent collection applies. Required if burn-rate alerting is enabled and the collection has no default policy.
- `signal_grouping` (Block List, Max: 1) Controls how individual time series are grouped into signals for alerting purposes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--signal_grouping))
- `slug` (String) Stable identifier for the SLO. Generated from `name` if omitted. Immutable after creation.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--definition"></a>
### Nested Schema for `definition`

Required:

- `objective` (Number) Target SLO percentage representing the desired availability (e.g. `99.9`).

Optional:

- `burn_rate_alerting_config` (Block List) Custom burn-rate alert definitions. If omitted, the system default burn rates are used. Only takes effect when `enable_burn_rate_alerting` is true. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--definition--burn_rate_alerting_config))
- `enable_burn_rate_alerting` (Boolean) Whether burn-rate alerting is enabled for this SLO.
- `time_window` (Block List, Max: 1) Rolling time window over which the SLO objective is evaluated. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--definition--time_window))

<a id="nestedblock--definition--burn_rate_alerting_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `definition.burn_rate_alerting_config`

Required:

- `budget` (Number) Percentage of the error budget that may be consumed during `window` before the alert fires. Must be between 0.0 and 100.0 exclusive.
- `severity` (String) Severity assigned when the burn rate fires. Must be `critical` or `warn`.
- `window` (String) Time window for the burn-rate calculation (e.g. `1h`, `6h`).

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String) Additional labels attached when this burn-rate alert fires. Can be used by notification policies to route different burn rates to different destinations.


<a id="nestedblock--definition--time_window"></a>
### Nested Schema for `definition.time_window`

Required:

- `duration` (String) Length of the evaluation window (e.g. `28d`, `24h`).



<a id="nestedblock--sli"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sli`

Optional:

- `additional_promql_filters` (Block Set) Additional PromQL label matchers applied to SLI queries via the `{{.AdditionalFilters}}` template variable. Used to narrow the metrics scope. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sli--additional_promql_filters))
- `custom_dimension_labels` (List of String) Additional labels exported from the underlying queries to group the error budget by. Available in PromQL templates via `{{.GroupBy}}`.
- `custom_indicator` (Block List, Max: 1) Error-ratio SLI defined by good/bad/total PromQL query templates. Mutually exclusive with `custom_timeslice_indicator`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sli--custom_indicator))
- `custom_timeslice_indicator` (Block List, Max: 1) Time-slice SLI that evaluates a PromQL query over fixed time slices against a threshold condition. Mutually exclusive with `custom_indicator`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sli--custom_timeslice_indicator))

<a id="nestedblock--sli--additional_promql_filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sli.additional_promql_filters`

Required:

- `name` (String) Prometheus label name to match.
- `type` (String) Matcher type (e.g. `=`, `!=`, `=~`, `!~`).
- `value` (String) Label value to match against using the chosen matcher `type`.


<a id="nestedblock--sli--custom_indicator"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sli.custom_indicator`

Required:

- `total_query_template` (String) PromQL query template measuring the total count of events. Required for error-ratio SLOs.

Optional:

- `bad_query_template` (String) PromQL query template measuring the count of bad events. Mutually exclusive with `good_query_template`.
- `good_query_template` (String) PromQL query template measuring the count of good events. Mutually exclusive with `bad_query_template`.


<a id="nestedblock--sli--custom_timeslice_indicator"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sli.custom_timeslice_indicator`

Required:

- `condition` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Condition used to classify each time slice as good or bad based on the query result. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sli--custom_timeslice_indicator--condition))
- `query_template` (String) PromQL query template evaluated against each time slice.
- `timeslice_size` (String) Size of each time slice evaluated by the query (e.g. `1m`, `5m`).

<a id="nestedblock--sli--custom_timeslice_indicator--condition"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sli.custom_timeslice_indicator.condition`

Required:

- `op` (String) Comparison operator between the query value and `value` (e.g. `gt`, `lt`, `eq`).
- `value` (Number) Threshold value compared against the query result using `op`.




<a id="nestedblock--signal_grouping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `signal_grouping`

Optional:

- `label_names` (List of String) Labels to group by. Series sharing the same values for these labels produce one signal. Defaults to no grouping (one signal per series).
- `signal_per_series` (Boolean) If true, treat each individual series as its own signal. Mutually exclusive with `label_names`.
